scenes.visual_prompt: 5 Stoic Lessons That Changed My Life
scenes.script_line: Marcus Aurelius ruled the Roman Empire for nearly two decades, and he left behind a journal that was never meant to be published.

scenes.visual_prompt: Marcus Aurelius sitting alone in a dimly lit Roman tent, writing in a leather journal by candlelight, dramatic chiaroscuro lighting, oil painting style
scenes.script_line: His private meditations became one of the most influential philosophy books ever written.

scenes.visual_prompt: sunrise over ancient Roman ruins columns
scenes.script_line: Lesson one. You have power over your mind, not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.

scenes.visual_prompt: A stoic philosopher standing calm amid a raging storm, dramatic lighting, bold illustration style with deep shadows
scenes.script_line: Most of what we suffer is not from the event itself, but from our judgment about it.

scenes.visual_prompt: person meditating on mountain peak at golden hour
scenes.script_line: The obstacle is the way. Start practicing today, and your life will never be the same.
